meta_description: This paper analyzes the fundamental tradeoffs between latency, reliability, and cost in LLM-enabled agentic workflows.
body_html:
The paper introduces performance models for both LLM and non-LLM agents that capture the relationship between computational effort and output quality. This analysis is crucial for understanding the fundamental tradeoffs between latency, reliability, and cost in LLM-enabled agentic workflows.

Performance Models for LLM and Non-LLM Agents

Performance models capture the relationship between computational effort and output quality. The models incorporate the impact of reasoning and output tokens for LLM agents using a parametric exponential reliability function.

Design of Sequential Workflows under Latency and Cost Constraints

Latency The paper studies the design of sequential workflows under latency constraints.

Cost The design also considers cost constraints.

Main Results and Contributions Main results include a water-filling token allocation policy and characterizations of optimal workflow reliability in terms of shadow prices.

source.title: Toward Reliable Design of LLM-Enabled Agentic Workflows: Optimizing Latency-Reliability-Cost Tradeoffs
source.summary: Researchers have developed performance models to analyze tradeoffs between latency, reliability, and cost in workflows composed of large language model (LLM) agents and conventional computational modules. The study aims to optimize these tradeoffs by designing sequential workflows under constraints on latency and cost. The findings include a water-filling token allocation policy and characterizations of optimal workflow reliability using shadow prices. This work seeks to improve the design of LLM-enabled agentic workflows.
